description: MIN and MAX return NULL on empty sets

columns.name: earliest
columns.note: MIN returns NULL on empty set

columns.name: latest
columns.note: MAX returns NULL on empty set

name: min_max_nullable
query_sql:
-- @name GetUserDateRange
-- @returns :one
SELECT MIN(created_at) AS earliest, MAX(created_at) AS latest FROM users
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, bio TEXT, age INTEGER, score NUMERIC, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly recognizes that MIN and MAX return NULL on empty result sets
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks MIN/MAX of NOT NULL columns as non-nullable
